CHAMONIX

The Coven's Alpine Adventure
Saturday 5 – Wednesday 9 September 2026
Chalet Soleil, Les Tines · 7 witches · 4 nights
Hiking · Spa · Fondue · Mont Blanc views · Jacuzzi · Rosé
↓ scroll ↓
Essentials Budget Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 Day 4 Day 5 Practical

The Essentials

Flights

Out: LX 353 · Sat 5 Sept · 08:55–10:35 · London → Geneva
Back: LX 348 · Wed 9 Sept · 14:50–16:35 · Geneva → London

Airport Transfer

Private minibus for all 7. Door-to-door, no waiting around at the airport. Approximately £160–200 each way for the vehicle = ~£25–30 per person each way. Basically the same as a shared shuttle but we leave immediately.

Providers: Mountain Drop-offs (mountaindropoffs.com) · Alpine Fleet (alpinefleet.com) · Journey ~1 hr 15 mins.

Accommodation

Chalet Soleil, Les Tines — 4 bedrooms sleeping 8. Two en-suite, two sharing a family shower room. Mont Blanc views from the deck, outdoor jacuzzi, underfloor heating, modern kitchen. Located in a quiet hamlet between Chamonix centre (~4 km) and Argentière. A nature trail from the chalet leads into town (~3 km walk).

Getting Around

Pick up a free Guest Card (Carte d'Hôte) from the Tourist Office or our accommodation. This gives us free bus and train travel within the valley. Download the Chamonix Mobilité app for live bus times.

Mont Blanc MultiPass

Covers the Flégère cable car, Montenvers cog railway, and more. Sales for summer 2026 open mid-April to early May at montblancnaturalresort.com. Buy online 3+ days ahead for the best price.

Budget

Spending money target: £500 per head. Here's how it stacks up.

Shared Costs (per person)

ItemCost ppNotes
Airport transfer (return)£25–30 ea wayPrivate minibus, split 7 ways
Supermarket shop£40Breakfasts, lunches, snacks, wine
Mont Blanc MultiPass£70–1251–3 day depending on usage

Per-Day Costs

ItemCost ppNotes
Dinner out – mid-range£40–60Main + wine + dessert
Dinner out – upmarket£65–90E.g. Cap Horn
Private chef dinner£60–903–4 courses incl. ingredients
QC Terme spa day£40–50Full day, robe, Aperiterme
QC Terme massage£40–7025 or 50 min session (optional)
Drinks & cafés£5–10/dayCoffee €3–4, wine €6–9
Mountain lunch£12–18Refuge or buvette

Realistic Totals

ScenarioTotal pp
Comfortable trip£400–500Within our £500 budget
Spa + massage + nice dinners£500–580Slight stretch, very doable

The £500 target is realistic. Cooking breakfast/lunch at the chalet and saving restaurant spend for 2–3 nice dinners out keeps things comfortable.

Day 1 — Saturday 5 September"Land & Get Our Bearings"

08:55
Fly LX 353, London to Geneva.
10:35
Land Geneva. Clear customs, meet private transfer driver.
~12:00
Arrive Chalet Soleil. Unpack, freshen up, fight over bedrooms, explore the chalet and deck.
13:30
Walk the nature trail from the chalet into Les Praz / towards Chamonix centre (~3 km, flat, pretty riverside path). Pick up our Guest Cards at the Tourist Office.
14:30
Mooch around Chamonix town. Browse the pedestrian streets, pop into the outdoor shops, get our bearings.
15:30
Late lunch at Munchie — one of the most popular spots in town. Trendy, great food, buzzy terrace. Swedish-run with a fun cross-over menu. Book ahead if we can.
18:00
Sunset drinks at Folie Douce terrace (check it's running for summer — if not, Josephine Brasserie on the pedestrian street is a gorgeous alternative with Mont Blanc views). Watch the sun drop behind the Aiguilles.
20:30
Option A: Head back to the chalet. Jacuzzi under the stars, cook something simple, save energy for tomorrow's hike.

Option B: Go wild in Chamonix. Rue des Moulins is the nerve centre — start at Le Jungle (tropical cocktails, DJs, buzzy), then Les Caves (luxe cocktail bar with table service that turns into a party later), and if we're still standing: BAM Karaoke Box — private karaoke rooms, signature cocktails, 45,000 songs. The coven has spoken. 🔮

Day 2 — Sunday 6 September"Big Hike Day"

The Hike: Lac Blanc

Difficulty
Moderate
Total Time
5–6 hours
Distance
~10 km loop
Elevation
+500m

Getting There from Les Tines

Walk 15 minutes from our chalet to the Flégère cable car station in Les Praz (or Bus Line 1, one stop, free with Guest Card). Cable car up to La Flégère at 1,877m — covered by MultiPass, ~8 minutes.

☀️ Why we're starting at 10am: Starting earlier means we'll hike in the cooler morning temperatures, avoid the Flégère cable car queue (it gets busy from late morning), and reach the lake before the afternoon clouds that often build around Mont Blanc in September. We'll also be back down comfortably before the last lifts close.

The Hike Step by Step

08:30
Breakfast at the chalet (croissants, fruit, coffee from our supermarket run).
09:30
Walk to Les Praz. Take the Flégère cable car up. Optional: take the Index chairlift even higher to shorten the walk by ~30 mins.
10:00
Start hiking. Trail heads north-east from Flégère, well signposted for Lac Blanc. First hour is a steady climb through alpine meadows with increasingly dramatic Mont Blanc views.
11:00
Final 30–40 minutes gets rockier with some scrambling and short ladder sections. Nothing dangerous but proper hiking boots are essential.
12:00
Arrive at Lac Blanc (2,352m). The reflection of Mont Blanc in the turquoise water is THE classic Chamonix photograph.
12:00–13:00
Picnic at the lake or eat at the Refuge du Lac Blanc — hot meals, drinks, snacks. ⚠️ CASH ONLY.
13:00
Head back. Either retrace to Flégère (2 hrs) and cable car down, OR continue west along the Grand Balcon Sud ridge towards Planpraz (~2 hrs of stunning ridge walking) and take the Brévent cable car down.
15:30
Cable car down. Walk or bus back to Les Tines.
16:00
Collapse on the deck. Jacuzzi. Rest. We've earned it.

Evening: Private Chef Dinner

19:00
Private chef arrives. 3–4 course alpine-inspired menu — charcuterie, trout, Reblochon, seasonal veg, knockout dessert. They handle everything including cleanup.
20:00
Sit down to eat.
Late
Drinks on the deck under the stars. Hot tub. Peak coven energy. ✨

Private chef options:

→ Jenna May Food — jennamayfood.com — Chamonix-based, charges for time + ingredients

→ Take a Chef — takeachef.com — Avg €92 pp for 3 courses, easy online booking

→ ChefMaison — chefmaison.com — From €59–194 pp for 4 courses

Book well in advance. The Hideaways Club concierge can also recommend and arrange the best private chefs for our chalet.

🎒 Pack for the hike: Layers, waterproof jacket, hiking boots (not trainers), sunscreen, sunglasses, water bottle, snacks, and CASH for the refuge.

Day 3 — Monday 7 September"Spa & Gorgeous Food"

10:00
Leisurely breakfast at the chalet. Enjoy the deck, the views, the lack of an alarm.
11:00
Bus or walk into Chamonix centre.
11:30
Arrive at QC Terme. Full-day pass (~€44–50) includes access to everything plus robes, towels, slippers, and the evening Aperiterme.

What's inside QC Terme:

→ Infinity pool overlooking the Bossons Glacier and Mont Blanc

→ 30+ wellness practices: saunas, hammam, Japanese baths, chromotherapy, Kneipp paths, salt rooms, hydrojets, waterfalls

→ Themed relaxation rooms across multiple floors

→ Optional massages: €44 for 25 mins, €78 for 50 mins (book in advance)

13:00
Wellness Lunch at QC Terme (€42) — a light, healthy buffet we eat in our robes. Glass of wine included.
13:00–16:00
Continue working through the spa circuit. Most people spend 4–5 hours. There's a LOT to explore.
16:30
Leave QC Terme. Head back to the chalet to change for dinner.
19:30
Dinner at Cap Horn — Rue des Moulins. One of the most popular restaurants in Chamonix. Stylish, buzzy, excellent seafood and international dishes with a Scandinavian edge. Mains €30–60. BOOK AHEAD.

Tip: Arrive when QC Terme opens to get the pools to ourselves. Mornings are much quieter. The infinity pool with the glacier view is best in daylight.

Day 4 — Tuesday 8 September"Wind Down with the Floral Forest Hike"

The Hike: Chalet de la Floria

Difficulty
Easy–moderate
Total Time
2.5–3.5 hrs
Distance
~5 km return
Elevation
+270m

Getting There from Les Tines

Walk out the front door. No bus, no cable car. We walk along the nature trail towards Les Praz (15 mins), cross the bridge near the golf course, and follow signs for Petit Balcon Sud / Chalet de la Floria into the forest.

The Hike Step by Step

11:00
Walk from our chalet along the nature trail towards Les Praz. Flat, easy, riverside.
11:15
Optional stop at Le Paradis des Praz — pretty woodland clearing with a café and stream.
11:30
Cross the Arve bridge near the golf course. Follow signs for Petit Balcon Sud / Chalet de la Floria. Steep at first but not technical — mostly a wide forest track.
12:15
Arrive at Chalet de la Floria (1,350m). A historic stone chalet covered in hundreds of colourful flowers, with a curved panoramic terrace overlooking the entire Mont Blanc massif.

🌸 Lunch on the Terrace:

→ Omelettes, quiche, tarts, salads, crozets with cheese

→ Wine, beer, and the local limeade (limonade citron vert — get it!)

→ ⚠️ CASH ONLY — no cards accepted

→ Kitchen open roughly 11:45am–3:00pm

12:30
Long, lingering lunch with views. We'll arrive just as the kitchen opens — perfect timing. This is the spot where we'll want to stay for ages.
14:00
Descend same way back (45 mins) or loop via a different forest path.
14:45
Back at the chalet. Final packing. Last jacuzzi session.

Evening: Farewell Dinner

15:30
Free afternoon — final shopping in Chamonix, or relax at the chalet.
19:30
Farewell dinner. Two great options:
→ Atmosphère — Beautiful terrace overlooking the Arve river. Excellent set menus, great wine list. Classic French. €40–70 pp.
→ La Maison Carrier — Converted Savoyard farmhouse. Antique alpine furnishings, crackling fireplace. Set menus from €20.

Book our farewell dinner in advance — both places are popular in September.

Day 5 — Wednesday 9 September"Go Home"

08:30
Breakfast. Final bits of packing. Tidy the chalet. One last look at Mont Blanc from the deck.
10:45
Private transfer to Geneva Airport. ~1 hr 15 mins drive.
~12:00
Arrive Geneva. Check in, security, duty-free.
14:50
Fly home. LX 348 to London, landing 16:35. 💔

Transfer timing: 10:45 departure gets us to Geneva by ~12:00 — nearly 3 hours before our flight. Plenty of time.

Practical Notes

What to Pack

September in Chamonix: daytime highs around 16–20°C in the valley, but significantly colder at altitude (can be below 5°C at 2,300m+). Pack layers, a lightweight waterproof, sturdy hiking boots (not trainers), sunscreen, sunglasses, and a warm fleece. Swimwear for QC Terme and the chalet jacuzzi.

Cash

Mountain refuges and buvettes are cash only (both Lac Blanc and Chalet de la Floria). Carry at least €30–50 per person for mountain food/drinks. France uses the Euro — £1 ≈ €1.16–1.19. Most restaurants and shops in town accept cards.

Restaurant Bookings

September is still busy. Book dinner reservations at least a week ahead, especially for Cap Horn, Munchie, Atmosphère, and La Maison Carrier. Most accept bookings via phone or the TheFork app. The Hideaways Club concierge can also help secure reservations for us.

Private Chef

Jenna May Food — jennamayfood.com — Chamonix-based, charges for time + ingredients
Take a Chef — takeachef.com — Avg €92 pp for 3 courses. Easy online booking.
ChefMaison — chefmaison.com — From €59–194 pp for 4 courses.
